settings.name: Flip Camera Feed
settings.info: Flip the camera feed/image horizontally (left ↔ right) or vertically (top ↔ bottom).
settings.label: Horizontal Flip

settings.name: Auto Focus Mode
settings.info: Controls autofocus behaviour. Manual: focus is fixed at the Lens Position set below. Continuous: the camera continuously adjusts focus automatically without user interaction.

childsettings.name: Lens Position
childsettings.info: Sets the lens focus distance in dioptres (1 / distance in metres). 0.0 focuses at infinity; 10.0 focuses at 10 cm. Higher values focus on closer subjects.
childsettings.label: Lens Position:

childsettings.name: Auto Focus Range
childsettings.info: Limits the distance range searched during autofocus. Normal: the standard range (may skip very close subjects). Macro: only the closest distances. Full: the entire focus range.

childsettings.name: Auto Focus Speed
childsettings.info: Controls how quickly the autofocus algorithm moves the lens. Fast reaches focus sooner but may be slightly less precise than Normal.

settings.name: Exposure- & Gain Compensation
settings.info: Auto: Automatic & continuous exposure and gain control (AEC/AGC — Auto Exposure Control / Auto Gain Control). Manual: Exposure time and analogue gain are set manually.

childsettings.name: Exposure Time
childsettings.info: Exposure time for the sensor to use, measured in microseconds.
childsettings.label: Exposure time

childsettings.name: Analogue Gain
childsettings.info: Amplification of the light signal directly at the image sensor, before digitisation. Similar to ISO on a DSLR camera: higher values brighten the image but increase noise. A value of 1 means no amplification (minimum supported by the sensor hardware).
childsettings.label: Gain factor

childsettings.name: Exposure Compensation
childsettings.info: Exposure compensation in EV steps (each step doubles or halves the brightness). Positive values make the image brighter, negative values darker. 0.0 is the default exposure.
childsettings.label: Value:

childsettings.name: AE Constraint Mode
childsettings.info: Constrains how the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) handles different brightness regions. Normal: no special constraint. Highlight: protects bright highlights from clipping, which may result in a darker overall image.
childsettings.warning: Both contrain modes 'Shadows' and 'Custom' require a configuration file and are therefore not supported by this webUI

childsettings.name: AE Exposure Mode
childsettings.info: Determines the range of exposure times the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) may choose from. Normal: balanced range. Short: favours short exposures (reduces motion blur, may increase gain/noise). Long: allows longer exposures (better in low light, increased motion blur risk).
childsettings.warning: Custom is currently disabled and requires a file to work and that feature is not in the webUI

childsettings.name: AE Metering Mode
childsettings.info: Controls which part of the frame the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) evaluates to set exposure. Centre Weighted: more emphasis on the centre. Spot: only the very centre. Matrix: the whole frame is measured equally.
childsettings.warning: Custom is currently disabled and requires a file to work and that feature is not in the webUI

childsettings.name: AE Flicker Mode
childsettings.info: When enabled, the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) avoids shutter speeds that cause banding under artificial lighting. Set the Flicker Period to match your mains frequency: 10000 µs for 50 Hz power grids (100 Hz flicker), 8333 µs for 60 Hz power grids (120 Hz flicker).
childsettings.label: Off/Manual

childsettings.name: AE Flicker Period
childsettings.info: The period of the lighting cycle in microseconds. For example, for 50 Hz mains lighting the flicker occurs at 100 Hz, so the period would be 10000 microseconds.
childsettings.label: Value in microseconds

settings.name: White Balance Mode
settings.info: Auto: Colour gains are continuously adjusted by the auto white balance algorithm (AWB). Manual: White balance is set manually via Colour Temperature.

childsettings.name: Colour Temperature
childsettings.info: Sets the colour temperature of the light source in Kelvin for manual white balance. Lower values (e.g. 2700 K) produce warm, amber tones; higher values (e.g. 6500 K) produce cool, bluish tones.
childsettings.label: Value:

settings.name: Brightness
settings.info: Adjusts the image brightness where -1.0 is very dark, 1.0 is very bright, and 0.0 is the default 'normal' brightness.
settings.label: Value:

settings.name: Contrast
settings.info: Sets the contrast of the image, where 0 means no contrast, 1.0 is the default 'normal' contrast, and larger values increase the contrast proportionately.
settings.label: Value:

settings.name: Saturation
settings.info: Amount of colour saturation, where 0 produces greyscale images, 1.0 represents default 'normal' saturation, and higher values produce more saturated colours.
settings.label: Value:

settings.name: Sharpness
settings.info: Sets the image sharpness, where 0 implies no additional sharpening, 1.0 is the default 'normal' level of sharpening, and larger values apply proportionately stronger sharpening.
settings.label: Value:

settings.name: Photo Resolution
settings.info: Sets the resolution used when capturing a still image, independent of the sensor mode.

options.label: This needs to be where the resolutions go

settings.name: Save RAW DNG
settings.info: When enabled the camera will save both a JPG and a RAW file from the same buffer. A download link for the DNG file will be added to the media gallery.
settings.label: Disabled/Enabled

settings.name: Live Stream Resolution
settings.info: Resolution used for the live stream. Can differ from recording resolution.
